question_template: How do I get a parking permit?
response: To request a parking permit in El Prat, visit the Municipal Services office with your vehicle documentation and proof of residence. The permit typically costs €50-100 per year and takes 5-10 business days to process. Permits are available for residents, residents' families, and businesses.

question_template: What are the parking zone rules?
response: El Prat has three parking zones: Zone A (downtown), Zone B (residential), and Zone C (peripheral). Zone A requires hourly payment Monday-Saturday 9am-8pm. Zones B and C allow 2-hour free parking. Parking permits exempt residents from all restrictions in their zone.

question_template: How much is a parking fine?
response: Parking fines in El Prat range from €30 for minor violations (expired meter) to €150 for blocking fire hydrants or handicapped spaces. Fines can be contested within 15 days at the Municipal Services office. Payment can be made online, by mail, or in person.

question_template: Where can I park long-term?
response: Long-term parking is available in municipal lots near the city center. Monthly rates typically range from €50-150 depending on location and demand. Some commercial lots also offer reasonable rates. Check the city website or call the parking authority for current availability.

question_template: What are the garbage collection days?
response: In El Prat, garbage collection runs on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday mornings. Please put bins out the evening before collection day. All organic waste must be in green bins, paper/cardboard in yellow bins, and plastic in blue bins.

question_template: How do I set up recycling?
response: Recycling in El Prat is mandatory. Contact the Environmental Services department to request color-coded bins (green for organics, yellow for paper, blue for plastic). Installation is free. You can also drop recyclables at the municipal collection center open Monday-Saturday 8am-6pm.

question_template: What items are not accepted in garbage bins?
response: Prohibited items include electronics, batteries, paint, chemicals, large furniture, and construction waste. These require special disposal. Take electronics and batteries to the municipal hazardous waste center. Schedule bulk item pickup by calling Environmental Services.

question_template: Where is the recycling center?
response: Most municipalities operate free recycling centers. Check your city's website for a location near you. Hours are usually Monday-Saturday, 8am-6pm. Many centers accept electronics, metal, glass, and hazardous waste in addition to standard recyclables.

question_template: What are the library hours?
response: El Prat Public Library is open Monday-Friday 9am-8pm, Saturday 10am-2pm, Sunday closed. Extended summer hours apply June-August. You can check the website or call ahead if visiting on a holiday.

question_template: How do I get a library card?
response: To obtain a free library card, visit any branch with a government ID and proof of address. Cards are issued immediately. Children under 14 can get a card with a parent's approval. Non-residents can apply for a €15 annual fee.

question_template: Can I reserve books online?
response: Yes! The library website allows online reservations. Log in with your library card number and password. Reserved books are held for 7 days from the notification date. You can renew items online or by phone if no other patrons are waiting.

question_template: What is the library book loan limit?
response: Most public libraries allow 20-30 items per cardholder simultaneously. Loan periods are typically 21 days for books, 14 days for media. First-time borrowers may have a lower limit that increases after on-time returns.

question_template: When are property taxes due?
response: El Prat property taxes (Impuesto sobre Bienes Inmuebles) are due in two installments: 50% by June 20 and 50% by November 20. You can pay online, by mail, or in person at the tax office. Late payments incur a 1.5% monthly surcharge.

question_template: How do I appeal my property tax assessment?
response: File a claim within 15 days of receiving your assessment at the Tax Appeals Office. Include documentation of comparable properties or recent appraisals. The review process takes 30-60 days. Contact the office for the forms and supporting documents needed.

question_template: What is the local business tax rate?
response: Business licenses in El Prat require annual renewal and vary by business type. Rates range from €200 for sole proprietorships to €1000+ for larger retail/manufacturing. Apply at the Business Registry office. Most applications process in 5-10 business days.

question_template: Are there tax deductions for home improvements?
response: Many municipalities offer tax deductions for home energy improvements, solar installations, or seismic upgrades. Check with your local tax office for current incentives and deadline dates. Documentation of contractor quotes and completion certificates is usually required.

question_template: How do I apply for a business license?
response: Visit the Business Registry at City Hall with your business plan, registration documents, and ID. The process takes 5-10 days. Online applications are also available on the city portal. License renewal is annual and costs €200-500 depending on business size.

question_template: Can I get a marriage license in the city?
response: Yes, civil marriages are performed by the Municipal Registry. Schedule an appointment at City Hall. Required documents: government ID, proof of residence, birth certificate, and divorce decree (if applicable). The ceremony is conducted in Spanish and administered by the Registrar.

question_template: How do I register a new vehicle?
response: Vehicle registration is handled by the Traffic Authority office. Bring ID, proof of Spanish residency, vehicle documentation, and proof of insurance. Registration normally takes 1-2 weeks and costs €150-300 depending on vehicle type. Plates are issued on-site.

question_template: What is required for a passport application?
response: Passport applications require government ID, proof of nationality, and current photos. Most municipalities process applications within 2-4 weeks. Spain is part of the Schengen Area, so passports are valid throughout Europe. Expedited service is available for a surcharge.

question_template: How do I file a complaint about city services?
response: File a formal complaint (reclamación) at the Citizen Services office with details of the issue, dates, and any relevant documentation. Complaints are investigated within 30 days. You can also file online through the city portal. A response is required by law.

question_template: How long does a complaint investigation take?
response: The city is required to acknowledge receipt within 3 days. A full investigation typically takes 20-40 days. Complex cases may take up to 90 days. You will receive a written response with findings and any corrective actions. Appeals must be filed within 30 days of the response.

question_template: Can I get compensation for a municipal service failure?
response: Yes, if the city is found liable for damages due to negligence or service failure. File a claim (reclamación de responsabilidad civil) with documentation of injuries, losses, or damages. Claims are reviewed within 3 months. If denied, escalate to administrative court.

question_template: What building permits do I need for renovations?
response: Minor interior renovations may not require permits, but structural changes always do. Submit plans to the Building Department with contractor quotes and architectural drawings. Review typically takes 15-30 days. Inspections are required during and after construction.

question_template: Is zoning approval needed for my home business?
response: Check the city zoning map to verify your property is in a zone allowing home businesses. If yes, apply for a home business license. If no, request a zoning variance. Variances require neighbor approval and municipal board review, which can take 60-90 days.

question_template: What is the best way to contact city hall?
response: Contact methods: call 934-790-600 (Monday-Friday 9am-2pm), visit City Hall (Av. Pau Casals 20), or submit an online form at elprat.cat. Response time for online inquiries is typically 5 business days. For urgent issues, call or visit in person.

question_template: Are there community events coming up?
response: El Prat hosts municipal festivals, farmers markets, and cultural events. Check the city calendar on elprat.cat for dates and details. Major annual events include the Spring Festival (May), Summer Concert Series, and Christmas markets. Subscribe to the city newsletter for updates.

question_template: How do I pay a parking meter?
response: Parking meters in El Prat accept coins (€0.50, €1, €2) or credit/debit cards. The app 'ParkEasy' also allows mobile payments. Rates are €1/hour in Zone A and free for 2 hours in Zones B & C. Permits exempt residents from all meter charges.

question_template: How do I report illegal dumping?
response: Report illegal dumping immediately to Environmental Services at 934-790-610 or via the city app. Include the location, description of dumped items, and if possible, photos. The city responds within 24-48 hours to remove debris and investigate.

question_template: Are there programs for children at the library?
response: Yes, the library offers story hour (Tuesdays 4pm), summer reading camps, and school research help. Kids can attend coding workshops and participate in reading challenges for prizes. Sign up on the website or ask at the front desk for a complete schedule.

question_template: What is the VAT rate in Spain?
response: Spain's standard VAT rate is 21%, reduced rate is 10%, and super-reduced rate (food, medicines) is 4%. Most municipal services are exempt from VAT. When paying city taxes, check the invoice to see if VAT applies based on the service type.
